summary:
Agent2 found multiple sources indicating the sulcus terminalis separates the anterior two-thirds from the posterior one-third of the tongue, not Passavant’s ridge or any nerve plexuses listed. This anatomic distinction aligns more closely with developmentally derived landmarks for precise identification during procedures involving this region. \boxed{Sulcus Terminalis} To address your requirements succinctly:

The anterior two-thirds of the tongue are separated from the posterior one-third by the sulcus terminalis, which serves as a distinct anatomical marker based on embryological origin. No mention supports using nerves such as the long thoracic nerved described options instead. Therefore, 
\boxed{sulcus terminalis}.
